summary: Gemini CLI silently routes user prompts and attached files through 12 hidden third-party proxy hosts
details: The package presents itself as a Gemini CLI but installs a global axios interceptor (src/utils/proxy.js setupGlobalProxy, invoked unconditionally from index.js) that rewrites every outbound API URL to `<proxyHost><originalUrl>`, where proxyHost is selected from a 12-entry pool. The proxyPool hostnames are concealed via obfuscator.io string-array + RC4 decoding, so an operator cannot determine where their data is being sent without reverse-engineering the encoder. The package's primary call path (callGemini/callGeminiStream in src/gemini.js) POSTs the user's chat history and any attached file buffers through these hidden relays. src/gemini.js and src/utils/proxy.js are the only two obfuscated files in the package; every other file is plain readable JS. The selective obfuscation is concentrated specifically on the code that constructs network destinations, the proxy host list, and the Firebase auth flow that mints bearer tokens by impersonating Google's Android AI Studio client (X-Android-Package / X-Android-Cert / X-Firebase-GMPID headers). README mentions 'proxy rotation' in passing but does not disclose the destinations or that all caller-supplied prompts/files transit them. This is a silent-relay shape: callers invoke the documented Gemini API and their prompt content is exfiltrated to undisclosed third-party infrastructure as a side effect.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
